title: Nairobi 5-Day Wildlife Safari
metaTitle: Nairobi 5-Day Wildlife Safari | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Nairobi, Kenya: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Nairobi National Park and David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust. Prices from $1594 per person (estimate).
highlights: Maasai Mara National Reserve (flight or long drive)
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Nairobi, Kenya — Mild, temperate climate for an equatorial city thanks to its altitude (1,795m) — highs around 24-26°C year-round, with two rainy seasons (March-May, October-December). June to October and January to February — the driest months, best for wildlife viewing as animals cluster around water sources. This itinerary blends Nairobi National Park, David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust, Maasai Market with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Nairobi National Park, David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust, Maasai Market
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Nairobi
itinerary.description: Land at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (NBO) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Nairobi National Park — The only national park bordering a capital city, with lions and giraffes minutes from downtown. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Nairobi: David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust & Giraffe Centre
itinerary.description: Morning visit to David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust — Elephant orphanage with daily public feeding visits. In the afternoon, continue to Giraffe Centre — Hand-feed endangered Rothschild giraffes at eye level. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Nairobi: Karen Blixen Museum & Maasai Market
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Karen Blixen Museum — Former farmhouse of the "Out of Africa" author. In the afternoon, continue to Maasai Market — Rotating open-air craft market for beadwork, textiles, and carvings. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Maasai Mara National Reserve (flight or long drive)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Maasai Mara National Reserve (flight or long drive), a popular side trip from Nairobi. Return to Nairobi in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Maasai Mara National Reserve (flight or long drive)

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Nairobi National Museum grounds or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to NBO for your departure flight.

travelTips: Most visitors treat Nairobi as a one-to-two-day gateway before flying into the Maasai Mara or another safari circuit.
Book David Sheldrick and Giraffe Centre visits for the morning — feeding times are limited.
Use a reputable, licensed safari operator for any game drives or park visits.
Carry cash in small denominations for markets and tips.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Neutral-toned clothing for safari (khaki, olive, tan)
A warm layer for cool mornings and evenings at altitude
thingsToDo: Karura Forest cycling and walking trails
Hot air balloon safari (Maasai Mara add-on)
thingsToAvoid: Walking around unfamiliar neighborhoods after dark.
Displaying valuables openly, especially in markets or traffic.
Booking safari add-ons through unlicensed street operators.
hiddenGems: Bomas of Kenya cultural dance shows
adventureActivities: Karura Forest cycling and walking trails
Hot air balloon safari (Maasai Mara add-on)
luxuryExperiences: Fly-in safari extension to the Maasai Mara
Private guided game drive with a naturalist
Suite stay at a Karen-area boutique lodge
honeymoonExperiences: Private sundowner at Nairobi National Park
Fly-in extension to a Mara luxury tented camp
localEtiquette: Greetings matter — a handshake and brief exchange of pleasantries before business is expected.
Dress modestly and conservatively outside safari lodges and upscale venues.
Tipping safari guides and drivers (10-15 USD per day) is customary.
localTransportGuide: Rideshare apps (Uber, Bolt) are the easiest and safest way to get around Nairobi for visitors. Traffic congestion is significant, especially at rush hour, so build in extra time. Most safari extensions from Nairobi are handled by a tour operator with a dedicated driver-guide.
bestTimeToVisit: June to October and January to February — the driest months, best for wildlife viewing as animals cluster around water sources.
weather: Mild, temperate climate for an equatorial city thanks to its altitude (1,795m) — highs around 24-26°C year-round, with two rainy seasons (March-May, October-December).
visaInformation: Kenya requires an electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) for nearly all visitors, including US, UK, EU, Canadian, Australian, and Indian nationals, applied for online before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~11.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Nairobi?
faqs.answer: Kenya requires an electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) for nearly all visitors, including US, UK, EU, Canadian, Australian, and Indian nationals, applied for online before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Nairobi?
faqs.answer: June to October and January to February — the driest months, best for wildlife viewing as animals cluster around water sources. Mild, temperate climate for an equatorial city thanks to its altitude (1,795m) — highs around 24-26°C year-round, with two rainy seasons (March-May, October-December).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Nairobi safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Nairobi is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Nairobi?
faqs.answer: The local currency is KES. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: It also works well for families — highlights include Giraffe Centre feeding.

reviews.author: Marco Larsen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Maasai Market and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Sofia Dubois
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: David Andersen
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Nairobi is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Nairobi National Museum grounds. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
